name: CoreAge Rx
description: CoreAge Rx offers LegitScript-verified compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide with free 2-day shipping to all 50 states. Backed by licensed physicians and 24/7 support, it delivers the most competitive pricing in the compounded GLP-1 space.
pros: Lowest per-month pricing in the compounded segment
cons: Compounded only — no brand-name Ozempic or Wegovy
Newer brand with fewer independent reviews than legacy players

name: Hims
description: Hims is a well-known telehealth platform offering compounded semaglutide to all 50 states. Its slick app, FSA/HSA acceptance, and frequent promotional pricing make it the go-to choice for budget-conscious buyers.
pros: Frequent promo codes for first-month discounts
Large, established brand with strong trust signals
cons: Text-only async consultation — no video visit
Price increases sharply after promo period
Support response times can lag

name: Ro
description: Ro Body pairs licensed physicians with both brand-name and compounded GLP-1 options. Available in 20 states, Ro's holistic care model — combining medication, nutrition, and coaching — sets the bar for comprehensive telehealth weight management.
cons: Only available in 20 states
Brand-name GLP-1s are expensive without insurance
Waitlist can slow onboarding

name: Found
description: Found combines prescription weight-loss medication with 1-on-1 coaching and registered dietitian access, starting at just $129/month — the lowest entry price among full-service GLP-1 providers. Available in 25 states.
pros: Registered dietitian included in every plan
cons: Only 25 states
Coaching model requires patient engagement
Less brand recognition than Hims or Ro

name: Henry Meds
description: Henry Meds is a no-frills telehealth platform focused on fast, affordable access to compounded GLP-1 medications. With quick onboarding and service in 30 states, it suits patients who want medication without the extras.
pros: No pressure to add coaching or extras
cons: Limited to 30 states
Minimal coaching or lifestyle support
Lower trust scores vs. established brands

name: Sesame
description: Sesame is a unique marketplace offering both video telehealth and in-person physician visits for GLP-1 prescriptions. Available in 10 states, it's the best option for patients who want face-to-face flexibility alongside telehealth convenience.
cons: Only 10 states — most limited availability
Pharmacy coordination can add friction
Lower support score vs. full-service providers

name: Calibrate
description: Calibrate is a premium metabolic health program pairing brand-name GLP-1 medications with year-long physician oversight, health coaching, and behavior change curriculum. At $349/month, it targets patients who want the gold-standard clinical experience.
pros: Full year of physician and coach access
cons: Most expensive option at $349/mo
Only 15 states
Brand-only — no compounded fallback

name: PlushCare
description: PlushCare is a telehealth platform that accepts most major insurance plans, making it the best path for patients who want to use insurance coverage for their GLP-1 prescription. Board-certified doctors available same-day.
cons: Insurance billing adds complexity
Out-of-pocket cost high without insurance
Value score lower due to brand-only pricing

name: Mochi Health
description: Mochi Health connects patients with board-certified obesity medicine specialists — not just general practitioners — for GLP-1 prescriptions and ongoing management. Ideal for patients with complex metabolic health needs.
pros: Obesity medicine specialists — not just GPs
Lab work integration for safety monitoring
cons: More limited state availability
Higher price than no-frills options
Waitlist for popular specialists

name: Noom
description: Noom is a psychology-based weight loss app using cognitive behavioral techniques to drive lasting behavior change — no medication required. At $17/month on an annual plan, it's the most accessible structured weight-loss program available.
cons: No medication — results depend on adherence
Lower effectiveness vs. GLP-1 providers
Mixed reviews on long-term retention

name: WeightWatchers
description: WeightWatchers combines its proven Points system with community workshops and, increasingly, GLP-1 clinical support. From $23/month digital to $99/month GLP-1 clinic, it offers the broadest tier range of any program on this list.
pros: Multiple price tiers for every budget
GLP-1 clinic option for medication seekers
cons: Lower effectiveness without medication tier
App UX feels dated vs. newer competitors
Mixed Trustpilot reviews (3.5/5)

name: Transparent Labs Fat Burner
description: Transparent Labs Fat Burner is a premium thermogenic supplement formulated with third-party tested, clinically dosed ingredients. Designed for serious gym-goers who want a clean, no-proprietary-blend fat burner backed by a money-back guarantee.
pros: Third-party tested for purity and potency
No proprietary blends — full label transparency
cons: Supplements are not FDA-approved weight loss drugs
Requires consistent gym routine to maximize results
Limited peer-reviewed clinical trials on the specific formula

name: MitoQ
description: MitoQ delivers a clinically studied form of CoQ10 that targets mitochondria directly, supporting metabolic energy and cellular health. Made in New Zealand with pharmaceutical-grade manufacturing standards.
cons: Premium price point compared to standard CoQ10
Metabolic benefits take weeks to notice
Not a direct weight loss product

name: Lean Hybrid Protein
description: Lean Hybrid Protein is a high-protein meal replacement powder with 25g of protein and minimal carbs per serving. Designed for weight loss through satiety, it doubles as an FSA/HSA-eligible supplement for eligible buyers.
pros: 25g protein per serving for high satiety
Low carb formula supports ketogenic diets
Versatile — works as meal replacement or post-workout
cons: Smaller brand with fewer independent reviews
Not a medication — results depend on overall diet
Flavor options are limited

name: Thorne Metabolic Health Bundle
description: Thorne's Metabolic Health Bundle combines NSF-certified, practitioner-grade supplements targeting blood sugar regulation, metabolism, and body composition. The gold standard for patients who want doctor-recommended supplement quality.
pros: NSF Certified — highest third-party verification standard
Bundle pricing saves vs. buying individual supplements
Highest trust score in the supplement category
Comprehensive metabolic support in one order
cons: Most expensive supplement option at $129/mo
Bundle is fixed — can't customize individual products
Premium price may not suit all budgets

name: Factor
description: Factor delivers fresh, chef-prepared meals dietitian-designed for weight loss — fully cooked and ready in under 2 minutes. With keto, calorie-smart, and protein-plus options, it's the top pick for busy people who want zero meal prep.
pros: Fully cooked meals — heat and eat in 2 minutes
Multiple diet plans including keto and high-protein
cons: Premium price vs. cooking at home
Fixed weekly menus with limited customization
Requires consistent subscription for best results

name: BistroMD
description: BistroMD was founded by a bariatric physician and features meal plans specifically designed for medical weight loss, including programs for diabetes, menopause, and gluten-free needs. The most clinically rigorous meal delivery option.
pros: Founded by a bariatric physician — highest medical credibility
Specialized plans for diabetes, menopause, and gluten-free
Highest trust score in the meal delivery category
Clinically calibrated macros and calorie targets
cons: Most expensive meal delivery option at $155/mo
Smaller menu variety vs. newer services
Delivery days may be limited in some areas

name: Nutrisystem
description: Nutrisystem is the original portion-controlled meal delivery system with a 30-year track record of clinically validated weight loss. Shelf-stable and fresh meal combos ship directly to your door with a structured daily eating plan.
pros: 30-year proven track record of weight loss results
Structured daily plan removes decision fatigue
Large volume of real-world user reviews
cons: Most expensive per-month at ~$360
Older UX and app experience vs. newer competitors
Shelf-stable meals are less gourmet

name: Green Chef
description: Green Chef is a USDA Certified Organic meal kit service with a dedicated Keto & Calorie Smart plan for weight loss. Pre-prepped organic ingredients arrive weekly with simple 30-minute recipes designed by professional chefs.
pros: USDA Certified Organic — highest ingredient standard
Dedicated keto plan for low-carb weight loss
cons: Requires cooking — not as convenient as fully prepared meals
Fewer meal options per week than competitors
Organic premium adds to cost

name: MyFitnessPal Premium
description: MyFitnessPal Premium is the world's most popular calorie tracking app with 200M+ users, a barcode scanner database of 14M+ foods, and advanced macro tracking. Premium unlocks meal plans, calorie goal guidance, and priority support.
pros: Largest food database — 14M+ foods with barcode scanner
Annual plan is extremely affordable at $7/mo
Integrates with 50+ fitness apps and wearables
Recipe importer for easy home meal logging
cons: Free version has significant feature limitations
Database relies on user submissions — accuracy can vary
No built-in coaching or accountability features

name: Lose It!
description: Lose It! is a focused calorie tracking app with a unique Snap-It food photo recognition feature and a clean, beginner-friendly interface. At $40/year, it's one of the best value calorie trackers for weight loss.
pros: Snap-It AI food photo recognition is highly accurate
Cleanest, most beginner-friendly UI in category
Detailed weekly and monthly progress reports
cons: Smaller food database than MyFitnessPal
Limited third-party app integrations
No coaching or human accountability features

name: Noom Med
description: Noom Med integrates GLP-1 medication access with Noom's proven cognitive behavioral therapy curriculum, 1-on-1 dietitian coaching, and dedicated care team — the most comprehensive psychology-plus-medication program available.
pros: Combines GLP-1 medication with CBT behavior change
Dedicated dietitian included in every plan
Highest support score in fitness app category
Integrated care team for medication and coaching
cons: Available in only 40 states — excludes AK, HI, ID, MT, NM, SD, VT, WV, WY, ND
More expensive than app-only alternatives
Requires commitment to both medication and coaching program

name: Fitbit Premium
description: Fitbit Premium unlocks advanced health analytics, guided workouts, sleep tracking insights, and personalized wellness reports for Fitbit device owners. At $10/month, it's the best activity-based weight management subscription.
pros: Best sleep tracking and analysis in the category
Seamless integration with all Fitbit wearables
Personalized daily wellness scores and recommendations
cons: Requires a Fitbit device — not standalone
Lower effectiveness score vs. dedicated weight loss apps
Feature set tied to Google/Fitbit ecosystem

name: Apple Fitness+
description: Apple Fitness+ is a guided workout subscription with 1,000+ on-demand classes spanning HIIT, yoga, strength, cycling, and more. Powered by Apple Watch metrics in real-time, it's the most polished workout app available.
pros: Highest UX score — best-in-class Apple design
Real-time Apple Watch metrics shown on screen
Family Sharing for up to 6 members
cons: Requires Apple Watch — excludes Android users
Not a dedicated weight loss or calorie tracking tool
Lower value score vs. cheaper alternatives

name: Eden
description: Eden offers flat-rate compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide through tryeden.com with no dose-based price hikes and no membership fees. Shipping is included and plans scale down when you commit to a multi-month package.
pros: Multi-month discounts bring cost down further
cons: Compounded only — no brand-name options
First-month pricing jumps to standard after intro period

name: Form Health
description: Form Health is an insurance-first obesity medicine clinic led by board-certified obesity medicine physicians paired with registered dietitians. It focuses on FDA-approved GLP-1s like Wegovy, Zepbound, and Mounjaro rather than compounded alternatives.
pros: Registered dietitian included in the care plan
Accepts most major insurance and Medicare
cons: Self-pay plan is $299/mo and does not include medication
No compounded option for cash-pay patients
Insurance coverage for GLP-1s can be denied

name: Zealthy
description: Zealthy bundles medical care, prescriber visits, and compounded semaglutide into a flat membership. Multi-month commitments bring per-month costs down to the low end of the market.
pros: As low as $151/mo semaglutide on multi-month plans
cons: $135/mo membership fee if not bundled
Membership fees are not covered by insurance
Quality of coaching varies by provider

name: LifeMD
description: LifeMD is a publicly traded telehealth company offering both compounded and brand-name GLP-1s through partnerships with Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly. Its Weight Management program includes provider consults, coaching, and free delivery.
pros: Direct partnerships with Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly
10% weight loss refund guarantee after 1 year
cons: $19/mo LifeMD+ membership charged separately
First-month promo pricing roughly doubles after intro
Customer service complaints common in reviews

name: Lavender Sky Health
description: Lavender Sky Health is a concierge-style GLP-1 clinic with access to ten compounding pharmacy partners and physician-led titration. Designed for patients who want flexible dosing and personalized medical supervision.
pros: Ten pharmacy partners gives supply flexibility
No monthly membership fee — pay per plan
cons: $25/mo treatment-plan fee on top of medication
Not available in every state
Smaller brand — less name recognition

name: Joi + Blokes
description: Joi (women's health) and Blokes (men's health) share a unified GLP-1 program offering compounded semaglutide, compounded tirzepatide, and Lilly Direct Zepbound. Plans include clinician consultations and health coaching.
pros: Compounded and Lilly Direct Zepbound options
First-month pricing cut roughly in half
cons: Quarterly billing — higher upfront commitment
$99/mo separate program fee for Zepbound plan
Not available in every state

name: Everlywell Weight Care+
description: Everlywell Weight Care+ pairs the at-home lab-testing company's infrastructure with compounded semaglutide and clinician oversight. Baseline labs are included, making it attractive to patients who want medical rigor.
cons: 3-month minimum commitment
Membership jumps to $139/mo after first month
Medication is $199/mo on top of membership

name: GoodRx Care for Weight Loss
description: GoodRx Care for Weight Loss is GoodRx's telemedicine weight-management subscription with Novo Nordisk partnership pricing on Ozempic and Wegovy. It's one of the cheapest routes to brand-name semaglutide in 2026.
pros: $199/mo introductory pricing for Wegovy and Ozempic
Trusted GoodRx brand with strong name recognition
cons: Intro pricing only lasts 2 fills — jumps to $349/mo
No compounded alternative
Telehealth subscription rises to $119/mo after intro

name: Fridays
description: Fridays (joinfridays.com) is a dose-inclusive GLP-1 telehealth clinic where price stays flat no matter how high your dose goes. Annual plans drop compounded semaglutide to as low as $150/month.
pros: Dose-inclusive — price never rises with higher doses
Nutrition coaching included in every plan
cons: Best pricing requires large upfront annual commitment
No partial refunds on multi-month plans
Compounded only for self-pay

name: Remedy Meds
description: Remedy Meds is a nationwide compounded GLP-1 clinic available in 49 states with flat-rate pricing and unlimited video visits included in the monthly fee. A solid mid-tier option with broad coverage.
cons: Not available in Louisiana
No insurance billing — cash pay only
No brand-name Wegovy or Zepbound option

name: Lemonaid Health
description: Lemonaid Health, a 23andMe company, offers compounded semaglutide and microdose options alongside brand Ozempic and Wegovy. Its microdosing-first approach emphasizes tolerability over max-dose escalation.
pros: $49/mo membership is one of the lowest platform fees
cons: Membership fee billed separately from medication
Microdosing may slow weight loss vs. standard dosing
Brand-name GLP-1s are expensive without insurance

name: Orderly Meds
description: Orderly Meds provides all-inclusive compounded GLP-1 plans with telehealth consultation, medication, syringes, shipping, and unlimited provider messaging bundled into one monthly fee. One of the cheapest entry points for semaglutide.
pros: Compounded semaglutide from as low as $83/mo
cons: Lowest price requires multi-month commitment
Newer brand with limited independent reviews
No brand-name options

name: Hers
description: Hers (the women-focused sister brand to Hims) offers compounded semaglutide as part of its Weight Loss Membership. Plans start at $199/month when paid upfront for 12 months, making it competitive for long-term commitments.
pros: Content geared toward perimenopause and hormonal weight gain
cons: Best pricing requires 12-month upfront payment
Membership jumps to $149/mo after first month
Support channels are primarily async

name: MEDVi
description: MEDVi is a telehealth provider offering both injectable and tablet formulations of compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide. Its flat monthly pricing and tablet alternatives stand out for patients who prefer avoiding needles.
pros: Tablet formulations for patients who dislike needles
cons: Does not bill insurance
Tablet options are more expensive than injection
Mixed customer-service reviews

name: Strut Health
description: Strut Health is a custom-compounding telehealth service offering both injectable and under-the-tongue (sublingual) semaglutide for men and women. One of the few providers with oral semaglutide drops and lozenges.
pros: No-needle option rare in the market
Option to fill at external pharmacies
cons: Sublingual absorption less proven than injection
Limited independent clinical data on oral forms
Not available in all states
